text.title: inc-runbook-as-markdown-tagged-steps Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for inc-runbook-as-markdown-tagged-steps. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Every bullet under `## Remediate` MUST end with exactly one of `<!-- agent:auto -->` or `<!-- agent:approval -->`. The SRE agent MUST execute `agent:auto` steps unattended, MUST refuse `agent:approval` steps until a signed approval token arrives, and MUST abort the runbook with a `runbook_invalid` error when any remediate step lacks a tag.
rule.rationale: A runbook without an explicit tag forces the agent to guess; published Kiro-style production deletions trace to "the runbook said it was OK to run" prose interpretations. A closed tag set makes the safety boundary mechanical.

rule.statement: The agent MUST resolve the runbook path from the alert's `runbook_url` annotation, MUST verify the file contains all four canonical sections, and MUST refuse all remediation (returning `runbook_missing` to the orchestrator) when the lookup fails. The agent MUST NOT invent or "search" for a runbook.
rule.rationale: Lookup-by-search lets the agent pick a wrong-but-plausible runbook from a similarly named service, which is how cross-service blast radius incidents start. A failed lookup is a feature: it pages the human to author the missing runbook.
